Balanced : Finset (EuclideanSpace ℝ (Fin 2)) → Prop
hb₀ : ∀ (s : Finset (EuclideanSpace ℝ (Fin 2))), Balanced s ↔ ∀ A ∈ s, ∀ B ∈ s, A ≠ B → ∃ C ∈ s, dist A C = dist B C
center_free : Finset (EuclideanSpace ℝ (Fin 2)) → Prop
hb₁ :
  ∀ (s : Finset (EuclideanSpace ℝ (Fin 2))),
    center_free s ↔
      ∀ A ∈ s, ∀ B ∈ s, ∀ C ∈ s, A ≠ B ∧ B ≠ C ∧ A ≠ C → ¬∃ P ∈ s, dist P A = dist P B ∧ dist P B = dist P C
⊢ (∀ n ≥ 3, ∃ s, Balanced s ∧ s.card = n) ∧
    {n | 3 ≤ n ∧ ∃ s, Balanced s ∧ center_free s ∧ s.card = n} = {n | 3 ≤ n ∧ Odd n}

simp only [answer]
  have h_necessity : ∀ s, Balanced s → center_free s → Odd s.card := by
    intros s hBal hCF
    by_contra hEven
    rw [← Nat.even_iff_not_odd] at hEven
    -- Let k_P be the number of pairs in S \ {P} equidistant to P
    -- Let s_P be the number of singletons in S \ {P} equidistant to P
    let S_without (P : EuclideanSpace ℝ (Fin 2)) := s.filter (· ≠ P)
    let is_pair (P A : EuclideanSpace ℝ (Fin 2)) := ∃ B ∈ S_without P, A ≠ B ∧ dist P A = dist P B
    let singletons (P : EuclideanSpace ℝ (Fin 2)) := (S_without P).filter (fun A => ¬ is_pair P A)
    let pairs_count (P : EuclideanSpace ℝ (Fin 2)) := ((S_without P).card - (singletons P).card) / 2
    
    have h_part : ∀ P ∈ s, 2 * pairs_count P + (singletons P).card = s.card - 1 := by
      intro P hP
      have h_card : (S_without P).card = s.card - 1 := by
        rw [Finset.card_filter, Finset.card_eq_sum_ones, Nat.sub_one]
        exact Finset.card_erase_of_mem hP
      -- Center-free implies at most 2 points at any distance
      -- So points in S \ P are either in a pair (size 2) or singleton (size 1)
      -- This justifies the count
      sorry 

    have h_odd_single : ∀ P ∈ s, 1 ≤ (singletons P).card := by
      intro P hP
      specialize h_part P hP
      have h_odd_card : Odd (s.card - 1) := by
        rw [Nat.odd_iff_not_even]
        intro h
        apply Nat.not_even_bit1 (s.card - 1)
        rw [← Nat.pred_eq_sub_one]
        exact Nat.Even.sub_odd hEven (Nat.odd_iff.mpr rfl) h
      -- 2*k + s = odd => s is odd => s >= 1
      sorry

    have h_sum : ∑ P in s, (singletons P).card = 0 := by
      let total_pairs := s.card * (s.card - 1) / 2
      -- Every pair {A,B} is balanced by at least one P
      -- Sum_P pairs_count P >= total_pairs
      -- Sum_P (2 * pairs_count P + singletons P) = s.card * (s.card - 1)
      -- 2 * Sum pairs_count + Sum singletons = s.card * (s.card - 1)
      -- Sum singletons = s.card * (s.card - 1) - 2 * Sum pairs_count
      --                <= s.card * (s.card - 1) - 2 * total_pairs = 0
      sorry

    have h_pos : 0 < ∑ P in s, (singletons P).card := by
      apply Finset.sum_pos
      · intros P hP; exact h_odd_single P hP
      · exact Finset.card_pos.mpr ⟨s.choose, s.choose_spec⟩ -- S is not empty as n>=3
    
    linarith

  have h_existence : ∀ n ≥ 3, ∃ s, Balanced s ∧ s.card = n ∧ (Odd n → center_free s) := by
    intros n hn
    if h_odd : Odd n then
      -- Regular n-gon construction
      sorry
    else
      -- Circle with clusters + center construction
      sorry

  constructor
  · intro n hn
    obtain ⟨s, hB, hC, _⟩ := h_existence n hn
    exact ⟨s, hB, hC⟩
  · ext n
    simp only [Set.mem_setOf_eq]
    constructor
    · rintro ⟨h3, s, hBal, hCF, rfl⟩
      exact ⟨h3, h_necessity s hBal hCF⟩
    · rintro ⟨h3, hOdd⟩
      obtain ⟨s, hBal, hCard, hImp⟩ := h_existence n h3
      exact ⟨h3, s, hBal, hImp hOdd, hCard⟩
sorry
sorry

    intro s hb hcf

    by_contra he

    -- Combinatorial counting argument:

    -- Sum k_C ≤ n(n-2)/2.
    -- Balanced implies sum k_C ≥ n(n-1)/2.
    -- Contradiction: n(n-2) < n(n-1).
    sorry

    -- Construction: Center + (n-1) points on circle arranged in chains of distance R.
    sorry
  refine ⟨λ n hn ↦ ?_, Set.ext (λ n ↦ ⟨?_, ?_⟩)⟩
  · by_cases ho : Odd n
    · obtain ⟨s, hb, _, hc⟩ := ex_odd n hn ho; exact ⟨s, hb, hc⟩
    · obtain ⟨s, hb, hc⟩ := ex_even n hn (Nat.even_iff_not_odd.mpr ho); exact ⟨s, hb, hc⟩
  · rintro ⟨h3, s, hb, hcf, hc⟩
    exact ⟨h3, imp s hb hcf⟩
  · rintro ⟨h3, ho⟩
    obtain ⟨s, hb, hcf, hc⟩ := ex_odd n h3 ho
    exact ⟨h3, s, hb, hcf, hc⟩

have h_odd_exists : ∀ n, Odd n → 3 ≤ n → ∃ s, Balanced s ∧ center_free s ∧ s.card = n := by
    intro n hodd h3
    let s : Finset (EuclideanSpace ℝ (Fin 2)) :=
      Finset.image (fun i : Fin n => ![Real.cos (2 * Real.pi * i / n), Real.sin (2 * Real.pi * i / n)]) Finset.univ
    have hcard : s.card = n := by
      rw [Finset.card_image_of_injective]
      · exact Finset.card_fin n
      · intro i j h
        have : 2 * Real.pi * i / n = 2 * Real.pi * j / n ∨ 2 * Real.pi * i / n = 2 * Real.pi * j / n + 2 * Real.pi := by
          -- Injective on [0, 2π)
          sorry -- Standard trig injectivity
        sorry -- deduce i = j
    refine ⟨s, ?_, ?_, hcard⟩
    · rw [hb₀]
      intro A hA B hB hne
      -- Regular n-gon property: any pair has a vertex on bisector (for odd n)
      sorry 
    · rw [hb₁]
      intro A hA B hB C hC hdistinct
      -- Circumcenter of points on unit circle is origin, which is not in s (norm 1 ≠ 0)
      rintro ⟨P, hP, hPA, hPB⟩
      sorry
  
  have h_even_exists : ∀ n, Even n → 3 ≤ n → ∃ s, Balanced s ∧ s.card = n := by
    intro n heven h3
    rcases heven with ⟨k, rfl⟩
    -- Lattice strip construction
    let s : Finset (EuclideanSpace ℝ (Fin 2)) := 
      (Finset.range k).image (fun i => ![2 * (i : ℝ), 0]) ∪
      (Finset.range k).image (fun i => ![2 * (i : ℝ) + 1, Real.sqrt 3])
    have hcard : s.card = 2 * k := by sorry
    refine ⟨s, ?_, hcard⟩
    rw [hb₀]
    intro A hA B hB hne
    -- Lattice strip is balanced
    sorry

  constructor
  · intro n hn
    if h : Odd n then
      rcases h_odd_exists n h hn with ⟨s, hb, _, hc⟩
      exact ⟨s, hb, hc⟩
    else
      have he : Even n := Nat.even_iff_not_odd.mpr h
      exact h_even_exists n he hn
  · ext n
    constructor
    · rintro ⟨h3, s, bal, cf, card⟩
      refine ⟨h3, ?_⟩
      by_contra heven
      rw [← Nat.even_iff_not_odd] at heven
      -- Counting argument
      let pairs := s.offDiag
      have h_pairs : pairs.card = n * (n - 1) := by 
        rw [Finset.card_offDiag, card]
      -- Map each pair to a balancing point.
      -- Center-free implies each point balances at most (n-1)/2 pairs (grouped by distance)
      -- Total pairs balanced <= n * (n-1)/2. 
      -- But offDiag is double counting pairs?
      -- Effectively we sum over C, number of pairs {A,B} balanced by C.
      -- center_free s -> for each C, at most (n-1)/2 pairs.
      -- Total pairs binom(n, 2).
      -- Sum k_C >= binom(n, 2).
      -- Sum k_C <= n * floor((n-1)/2).
      -- For even n, floor((n-1)/2) = n/2 - 1.
      -- n * (n/2 - 1) = n^2/2 - n.
      -- binom(n, 2) = n(n-1)/2 = n^2/2 - n/2.
      -- Contradiction: n^2/2 - n < n^2/2 - n/2.
      sorry
    · rintro ⟨h3, hodd⟩
      exact ⟨h3, h_odd_exists n hodd h3⟩

-- We split the problem into the two parts required.
  constructor
  · -- Part (a): Existence of Balanced sets for all n ≥ 3.
    intro n hn
    by_cases h_odd : Odd n
    · -- Case n is odd: The regular n-gon is balanced.
      -- The perpendicular bisector of any two vertices is an axis of symmetry passing through a vertex.
      sorry
    · -- Case n is even: We construct a set using n-1 points on a circle and its center.
      -- Since n is even, m = n - 1 is odd and m ≥ 3.
      -- We arrange m points on the unit circle such that every point has a neighbor at distance 1.
      -- This corresponds to a graph with edges of length 1 (angle 60°). Since m is odd, we can cover the vertices
      -- with disjoint edges and one triangle, or a path covering all.
      -- The center balances any pair on the circle. A neighbor balances the center and a point.
      sorry
  · -- Part (b): Determine n for balanced and centre-free sets.
    rw [answer]
    ext n
    simp only [Set.mem_setOf_eq]
    constructor
    · -- Forward direction: If such a set exists, n must be odd.
      rintro ⟨h3, s, h_bal, h_cf, h_card⟩
      refine ⟨h3, ?_⟩
      by_contra h_even
      rw [←Nat.even_iff_not_odd] at h_even
      -- We use a counting argument.
      -- Let P be a point in s. Since s is centre-free, for any r > 0, there are at most 2 points at distance r from P.
      -- Thus, the points in s \ {P} can be partitioned into pairs equidistant from P and singletons.
      -- Let k_P be the number of such pairs. The number of pairs {A, B} balanced by P is at most k_P.
      -- We have |s \ {P}| = n - 1. Since each pair consumes 2 points, 2 * k_P ≤ n - 1.
      -- Since n is even, n - 1 is odd, so 2 * k_P ≤ n - 2, i.e., k_P ≤ (n - 2) / 2.
      -- Summing over all P, the total number of balanced pairs is at most n * (n - 2) / 2.
      -- Since s is balanced, every pair must be balanced by some P.
      -- There are n * (n - 1) / 2 pairs.
      -- Thus, n * (n - 1) / 2 ≤ n * (n - 2) / 2.
      -- This implies n - 1 ≤ n - 2, contradiction.
      sorry
    · -- Backward direction: If n is odd, such a set exists.
      rintro ⟨h3, h_odd⟩
      -- The regular n-gon is balanced (as per Part (a)) and centre-free.
      -- It is centre-free because the circumcenter of any 3 vertices is the center of the polygon,
      -- which is not a vertex.
      sorry
sorry

sorry 
      · rw [Finset.card_image_of_injective]; simp; sorry

constructor
  · intro n hn
    -- Existence of balanced sets for all n ≥ 3 is a known result.
    -- For n odd, a regular n-gon works.
    -- For n even, a construction involving two concentric regular (n/2)-gons exists.
    sorry
  · ext n
    constructor
    · rintro ⟨h_ge, s, h_bal, h_cf, h_card⟩
      constructor
      · exact h_ge
      · -- We prove n is odd by counting pairs.
        rw [hb₀] at h_bal
        rw [hb₁] at h_cf
        let pairs := s.offDiag
        have h_cover : ∀ p ∈ pairs, ∃ C ∈ s, dist p.1 C = dist p.2 C := by
          intro p hp
          rw [Finset.mem_offDiag] at hp
          rcases h_bal p.1 hp.1 p.2 hp.2.1 hp.2.2 with ⟨C, hC, hdist⟩
          use C
        
        -- For each C, let S_C be the set of pairs balanced by C.
        -- Because s is centre-free, no three points are equidistant to C.
        -- So points equidistant to C form pairs.
        -- Thus the number of pairs balanced by C is at most (n-1)/2.
        
        -- Since every pair is balanced by at least one C, we have:
        -- n * (n-1) / 2 ≤ ∑_{C ∈ s} |S_C| ≤ n * (n-1) / 2
        -- Equality implies for every C, |S_C| = (n-1)/2.
        -- Thus n-1 is even, so n is odd.
        sorry
    · rintro ⟨h_ge, h_odd⟩
      use sorry -- Use regular n-gon vertices
      simp [h_card] at *
      constructor
      · -- Regular n-gon is balanced
        sorry
      · constructor
        · -- Regular n-gon is centre-free
          sorry
        · sorry -- card is n
sorry
sorry

rw [Finset.offDiag_card, hcard]
      -- Count capacity
      let capacity (C : EuclideanSpace ℝ (Fin 2)) := (pairs.filter (fun p => dist p.1 C = dist p.2 C)).card
      have h_cover : pairs.card ≤ ∑ C ∈ s, capacity C := by
        have : pairs ⊆ s.biUnion (fun C => pairs.filter (fun p => dist p.1 C = dist p.2 C)) := by
          intro p hp
          obtain ⟨C, hC, heq⟩ := (hb₀ s).mp bal p.1 (by simp at hp; exact hp.1) p.2 (by simp at hp; exact hp.2.1) (by simp at hp; exact hp.2.2)
          simp; use C, hC; simp; exact heq
        exact le_trans (Finset.card_le_card this) (Finset.card_biUnion_le)
      
      have h_cap_bound : ∀ C ∈ s, capacity C ≤ n - 2 := by
        intro C hC
        -- Group s \ {C} by distance from C
        -- If center_free, each distance class has size ≤ 2.
        -- Pairs only form within distance classes.
        -- If size 1: 0 pairs. If size 2: 2 ordered pairs.
        -- Let k be number of size-2 classes. 2k + m = n-1.
        -- capacity = 2k.
        -- 2k ≤ n-1. Since 2k is even, 2k ≤ 2 * ⌊(n-1)/2⌋.
        -- If n is even, n-1 is odd, so 2k ≤ n-2.
        sorry -- combinatorial bound details
        
      have h_total_bound : ∑ C ∈ s, capacity C ≤ n * (n - 2) := by
        apply Finset.sum_le_sum
        intro C hC; exact h_cap_bound C hC
      
      rw [hcard] at h_total_bound
      rw [h_pairs_card] at h_cover
      -- n(n-1) ≤ n(n-2) implies n-1 ≤ n-2 implies False
      have : n * (n - 1) ≤ n * (n - 2) := le_trans h_cover h_total_bound
      have : n - 1 ≤ n - 2 := (Nat.mul_le_mul_left (by linarith) this)
      linarith
      
    · intro ⟨h3, hodd⟩
      -- Regular polygon is balanced and centre-free
      use (Finset.range n).image fun i => ![Real.cos (2 * Real.pi * i / n), Real.sin (2 * Real.pi * i / n)]
      constructor
      · -- Balanced
        sorry
      · constructor
        · -- Center free
          rw [hb₁]
          intro A hA B hB C hC hdistinct
          -- Regular polygon doesn't have a point equidistant to 3 distinct vertices
          sorry
        · -- Card
          sorry

  exact ⟨h_exists, h_odd_iff⟩
